description:
You are the release manager and must prepare deployment summary files from a
  small set of versioned release folders that already exist in
  /home/user/releases.  The job involves reading several text files, performing
  data-transformation steps in parallel where it makes sense, and writing three
  new artefacts in /home/user/deployment/summary.

  1.  Input layout (already present):
      /home/user/releases/1.2.0/artifacts.txt
      /home/user/releases/1.2.0/release_notes.txt

      /home/user/releases/1.3.0/artifacts.txt
      /home/user/releases/1.3.0/release_notes.txt

      /home/user/releases/2.0.0-beta/artifacts.txt
      /home/user/releases/2.0.0-beta/release_notes.txt

      Each *artifacts.txt* contains one artefact filename per line.
      Each *release_notes.txt* is a single-line sentence.

  2.  What you must create (target layout):
      /home/user/deployment/summary/artifacts_summary.json
      /home/user/deployment/summary/deployments.csv
      /home/user/deployment/summary/transform.log

  3.  Required content of artifacts_summary.json
      • The file must be valid UTF-8 JSON.
      • It must be a JSON array with one object per version, ordered
        1.2.0, 1.3.0, 2.0.0-beta.
      • Each object MUST have exactly the following two keys:
        "version"  -> a string, the folder name.
        "artifacts"-> an array of strings containing the artefact filenames
                      exactly as they appear in the corresponding artifacts.txt.
      • Pretty-print exactly as shown below (four spaces for indentation and a
        trailing newline):

        [
            {
                "version": "1.2.0",
                "artifacts": [
                    "app-1.2.0.jar",
                    "lib-1.2.0.so",
                    "docs-1.2.0.zip"
                ]
            },
            {
                "version": "1.3.0",
                "artifacts": [
                    "app-1.3.0.jar",
                    "lib-1.3.0.so",
                    "docs-1.3.0.zip",
                    "cli-1.3.0.tar.gz"
                ]
            },
            {
                "version": "2.0.0-beta",
                "artifacts": [
                    "app-2.0.0-beta.jar",
                    "lib-2.0.0-beta.so",
                    "docs-2.0.0-beta.zip",
                    "cli-2.0.0-beta.tar.gz",
                    "migrator-2.0.0-beta.sh"
                ]
            }
        ]

  4.  Required content of deployments.csv
      • A UTF-8 file with UNIX line endings and **exactly four** lines.
      • Comma-separated values with NO spaces.
      • Header followed by one line per version in the same order as the JSON.
        version,total_artifacts,note_length
        1.2.0,3,40
        1.3.0,4,45
        2.0.0-beta,5,55
      • The file must end with a trailing newline.

  5.  Required content of transform.log
      • Plain-text, UNIX line endings, three lines, each ending with \n.
      • First line must be: START
      • Second line must be: artifacts_summary.json:OK
      • Third  line must be: deployments.csv:OK

  6.  Parallelism requirement  
      The heavy work (reading the three release folders, extracting artefacts and
      release-note statistics) must be carried out with at least one command that
      explicitly enables parallel execution (e.g. GNU `parallel`, `xargs -P`, or
      `find ... -exec ... +`).  You do NOT need to show the commands here, but
      the transform.log file must exist exactly as described, implying that the
      parallel step succeeded.

  7.  Additional constraints
      • Create the directory /home/user/deployment/summary if it does not yet
        exist.  All three output files must be placed inside it.
      • Overwrite any existing files of the same name.
      • Do not alter or delete the original files under /home/user/releases.
      • Only the exact content and file names given above will be accepted by
        automated tests.

  Once everything is generated with the required formatting, your work is
  complete.  (You do not need to display the file contents in the console,
  though doing so will not harm the evaluation.)
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing input files (these are already on disk before the agent starts) -->
  <initial_files>
    <file path="/home/user/releases/1.2.0/artifacts.txt">
app-1.2.0.jar
lib-1.2.0.so
docs-1.2.0.zip
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/releases/1.2.0/release_notes.txt">
Bug fixes and performance improvements.
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/releases/1.3.0/artifacts.txt">
app-1.3.0.jar
lib-1.3.0.so
docs-1.3.0.zip
cli-1.3.0.tar.gz
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/releases/1.3.0/release_notes.txt">
Added new CLI support and minor enhancements.
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/releases/2.0.0-beta/artifacts.txt">
app-2.0.0-beta.jar
lib-2.0.0-beta.so
docs-2.0.0-beta.zip
cli-2.0.0-beta.tar.gz
migrator-2.0.0-beta.sh
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/releases/2.0.0-beta/release_notes.txt">
Major release beta featuring new architecture overhaul.
    </file>
  </initial_files>

  <!-- Expected output that the agent must create -->
  <expected_output>
    <file path="/home/user/deployment/summary/artifacts_summary.json" mode="0644">
[
    {
        "version": "1.2.0",
        "artifacts": [
            "app-1.2.0.jar",
            "lib-1.2.0.so",
            "docs-1.2.0.zip"
        ]
    },
    {
        "version": "1.3.0",
        "artifacts": [
            "app-1.3.0.jar",
            "lib-1.3.0.so",
            "docs-1.3.0.zip",
            "cli-1.3.0.tar.gz"
        ]
    },
    {
        "version": "2.0.0-beta",
        "artifacts": [
            "app-2.0.0-beta.jar",
            "lib-2.0.0-beta.so",
            "docs-2.0.0-beta.zip",
            "cli-2.0.0-beta.tar.gz",
            "migrator-2.0.0-beta.sh"
        ]
    }
]
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/deployment/summary/deployments.csv" mode="0644">
version,total_artifacts,note_length
1.2.0,3,40
1.3.0,4,45
2.0.0-beta,5,55
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/deployment/summary/transform.log" mode="0644">
START
artifacts_summary.json:OK
deployments.csv:OK
    </file>
  </expected_output>
